retrospective.harder_than_needed: The project's one-import-per-test-file convention (a single top-of-file `await import(...)` after the idb-keyval mock is registered) combined with module-level singletons (`_loaded`/`_loadStarted`) made the pre-impl commitment VQ-S2-009 (assert loaded-flag false BEFORE the async load resolves) untestable within the shared test file — earlier tests already trigger and resolve the load, and there is no reset hook. Only the true-after state is assertable. The store LOGIC is correct; the gap is purely a test-convention constraint, carried forward as VQ-S2-013 for verifier judgment rather than worked around with a fragile vi.resetModules().
retrospective.surprised_by: The pre-impl commitment VQ-S2-009 asked for a test proving isOutboundJoinRequestsLoaded() is false before the initial async IDB load resolves and true after. That could not be honored as written: _loaded/_loadStarted are module-level singletons and this test file does a single top-of-file `await import(...)` (the project's mocked-idb-keyval convention), so by the time the new reactive-layer describe block runs, earlier tests in the same file have already triggered and resolved the load with no exposed reset. dev documented this in-file and settled for a true-after-load assertion only. I did not treat this as a defect — it's a structural consequence of the one-import-per-file test convention, not a design gap in the store — but recorded it as post-impl question VQ-S2-013 so a verifier can judge whether an isolated fresh-import test file would be worth adding.

absorbed_from.note: Flagged the loaded-flag false-then-true test as infeasible within the shared-module-import test file (module singleton, no reset hook); settled for a documented true-after-load-only assertion per the task's explicit fallback instruction.
